commercial_bias.name: 🛒 Commercial Bias
commercial_bias.description: Search results dominated by product pages, affiliate blogs, and ads instead of genuine information
example_searches: iphone vs samsung which is better
commercial_bias.antidote: Try searching for independent reviews, forum discussions, or academic comparisons instead of 'best' or 'top' lists

echo_chamber.name: 🔄 Echo Chamber
echo_chamber.description: Searching only for information that confirms existing beliefs while avoiding opposing views
example_searches: why python is better than java
proof that climate change is fake
why android is better than iphone
echo_chamber.antidote: Search for the opposite viewpoint. If you searched 'why X is bad', also search 'why X is good'

recency_bias.name: ⏰ Recency Bias
recency_bias.description: Only looking at recent content while ignoring valuable older knowledge and historical context
recency_bias.antidote: Remove the year from your search. Look for timeless knowledge, classic articles, and historical perspectives

authority_bias.name: 🏛️ Authority Bias
authority_bias.description: Only trusting big brand sources and institutions while ignoring independent researchers and smaller voices
example_searches: what does WHO say about diet
authority_bias.antidote: Check what independent researchers, smaller universities, and citizen scientists have found. Big names aren't always right

negativity_bias.name: 😨 Negativity Bias
negativity_bias.description: Focusing on dangers, risks, and negative outcomes while ignoring positive possibilities
negativity_bias.antidote: Balance your research. Also search for success stories, positive outcomes, and solutions

popularity_bias.name: 📊 Popularity Bias
popularity_bias.description: Only looking at mainstream popular opinions while ignoring niche and minority viewpoints
popularity_bias.antidote: Search for hidden gems, underrated options, and niche alternatives. The most popular isn't always the best for you

confirmation_bias.name: ✅ Confirmation Bias
confirmation_bias.description: Actively seeking information that supports what you already believe
example_searches: proof that cold showers are healthy
studies that prove intermittent fasting works
confirmation_bias.antidote: Search for counter-arguments too. Science advances by trying to disprove theories, not just confirm them

geographic_bias.name: 🌍 Geographic Bias
geographic_bias.description: Search results biased toward your country or region, missing global perspectives
geographic_bias.antidote: Remove geographic filters. See how other countries approach the same topic. You might find better solutions
